roadmap.name: Bounded Retry Subprocess Wrapper
roadmap.why_first: It becomes the chokepoint for zip/unzip/sha256sum/python3-S/find/node and every future internal tool invocation. Without it, other tools can still hang or bypass failure learning.
required_features: timeout_seconds required; default 30s for sandbox-heavy operations
max_attempts with bounded backoff and no unbounded retry loops
kill process group on timeout where supported
capture stdout/stderr tail with byte limits
failure learning registry hook with recurrence_key
method reliability router lookup before execution
negative fixture proving forbidden unbounded unzip is denied
positive_fixtures: sha256sum small file succeeds under budget
python3 -S helper succeeds and emits receipt
zip central-directory listing succeeds via preferred method
negative_fixtures: unzip -tqq full archive denied by forbidden method
stdout/stderr over byte budget truncated with hash

roadmap.name: Atomic JSON Writer / State Commit Primitive
roadmap.why: Every registry, receipt, handoff, tracker, and state export needs one safe write primitive. It should replace ad hoc json.dump/open patterns.
required_features: JSON canonicalization and schema validation hook
fsync file before replace and fsync parent directory on POSIX
negative_fixtures: absolute path outside runtime root blocked

roadmap.name: Registry Mutation Guard Retrofit
roadmap.why: Registry mutation is high blast-radius. Existing engine advertises good invariants but predates CDR and has a stale hard-coded root that must be removed or governed.
required_features: all writes routed through Atomic JSON Writer
runtime-root binding from verified boot header/path map
exclusive lock with timeout and stale-lock policy
schema and relationship validation before commit
negative fixtures for stale root, missing idempotency key, broken reference, and cycle insertion

roadmap.name: Archive Validator / ZipSlip Gate
roadmap.tier: CDR_HIGH initially, promote to CDR_CRITICAL if it gates authority exports
roadmap.why: Archive validation is already a recurring failure domain. The new tool must unify CRC/header tests, unsafe path scans, duplicate detection, central-directory inventory, and selective extraction smoke without forbidden full recursive validation.
required_features: absolute path, dot-dot, drive-letter, NUL, and symlink traversal rejection
CRC/header test using bounded native or Node/Python method
central directory inventory with byte/file limits

roadmap.name: Session State Exporter Retrofit
roadmap.why: It is less foundational than process and write primitives but critical for continuity. It should inherit the subprocess wrapper and atomic writer rather than inventing its own safety checks.
required_features: negative fixtures for wrong root, vendor-root leak, malformed export, and missing boot authority

roadmap.name: Tool Envelope Compiler / Per-Action Gate Generator
roadmap.why: After core primitives exist, every OS command/work order should be generated from a typed envelope instead of hand-authored shell snippets.
required_features: deny untyped shell execution in governed stages
generate fixtures for each operation family

roadmap.name: Uniform Failure Learning Capture Hook
roadmap.why: R21 already provides the gate; this hook makes all wrappers write compatible learning packets automatically.

roadmap.name: Runtime Path Authority Resolver
roadmap.why: Several older tools still risk stale root assumptions. A shared resolver should supply current root, boot authority ZIP, work directory, receipt paths, and export targets from artifacts only.

next_recommended_stage.objective: Build ADS, schemas, command envelope, decision packet contract, failure learning interface, and fixture matrix for the bounded subprocess wrapper only. Do not implement broad retrofits in the same turn.
next_recommended_stage.stop_condition: Stop after ADS/contract/fixture plan is written and verified; implementation comes in Stage2.
